steps.observed: Dashboard loaded with seeded tasks across all columns

steps.expected: All task cards filtered out, empty state displayed
steps.observed: Board shows 'No matching tasks' heading with search icon, description text 'No tasks match the current filters. Try adjusting or clearing your filters.', and a 'Clear Filters' button
Component correctly detects active search filter and shows filter-specific empty state with clear button (data-testid='clear-filters-button')

evidence.network: Search filtering is client-side via applyClientFilters(), no network calls during filter

findings: EmptyState component handles two modes: with active filters (shows search icon, 'No matching tasks', 'Clear Filters' button) and without (shows clipboard icon, 'No tasks yet')
KanbanBoard.tsx correctly passes hasActiveFilters and onClearFilters props to EmptyState
hasActiveFilters check covers: projectId, status, agentId, and search filters
Component has proper accessibility: role='status', aria-live='polite'

steps.action: Set network route to delay API calls by 5000ms
steps.observed: Loading spinner and 'Loading board...' text displayed while API requests are in flight
Screenshot shows centered spinner (CSS animation) with 'Loading board...' text, data-testid='loading-state'

steps.action: Verify loading state disappears after API responds
steps.observed: After 5s delay route removed, board loads normally with all task data visible

evidence.network: API requests delayed 5000ms via agent-browser network route --delay 5000

findings: LoadingState shows centered spinner with CSS @keyframes animation and 'Loading board...' message
Proper accessibility: role='status', aria-live='polite', aria-label matches message
KanbanBoard renders LoadingState when isLoading is true from useTasks hook
useTasks hook uses React Query with refetchInterval: 5000ms and retry: 2 (from QueryClient defaults)

steps.action: Navigate to about:blank to clear React Query cache
Set network route to abort http://localhost:3200/api/health and http://localhost:3200/api/tasks
steps.observed: Network routes configured to abort both health and tasks endpoints

steps.expected: Dashboard shows error state with retry button
steps.observed: Error state displayed with: warning icon, 'Connection Error' heading (red), error details text, and 'Retry connection' button (data-testid='retry-button')

steps.action: Click 'Retry connection' button while routes still blocking
steps.expected: Retry triggered but still shows error
steps.observed: Retry button clickable, page remains in error state because API routes are still blocked

steps.action: Remove network route blocks and click retry
steps.expected: Dashboard recovers and shows task data
steps.observed: After removing route blocks, retry succeeds and board loads with all task data visible

evidence.network: API requests to /api/health and /api/tasks aborted via network route --abort to simulate API unavailability

findings: KanbanBoard checks both healthError and tasksError to determine error state
ErrorState rendered with onRetry={handleRetry} which calls both refetchHealth() and refetchTasks()
isRetrying state managed with Promise.all().finally() pattern
Different error details for health vs tasks errors: health error says 'Unable to connect to API server', tasks error says 'Failed to load tasks'
useApiHealth hook has retry: 1 and 5s refetchInterval; QueryClient has retry: 2 globally
ErrorState component: warning icon, 'Connection Error' heading, customizable details, Retry button with disabled/isRetrying state

BoardStates.test.tsx has comprehensive tests: renders default/custom messages, retry button present/absent, onRetry click handler, disabled when isRetrying, accessibility (role='alert', aria-live='assertive')

steps.observed: Kanban columns switch from horizontal row to vertical stack (flexDirection: column)
Board title smaller (20px vs 24px), columns stacked vertically with 12px gap, filters still accessible at top
All 6 columns (backlog, ready, in_progress, review, done, blocked) visible in vertical scroll
No console errors on mobile viewport

evidence.network: No network issues at mobile viewport

findings: isMobile state detected via window.innerWidth < 768 with resize event listener
When isMobile: flexDirection changes from 'row' to 'column', gap from 16px to 12px, fontSize from 24px to 20px
KanbanColumn receives isMobile prop for column-level responsive adjustments
DashboardContent uses maxWidth: 100% with className='dashboard-container'
Filters component uses flex-wrap for responsive filter bar layout

frictions.description: Capturing the loading state (VAL-DASH-010) required network delay simulation via agent-browser network route --delay 5000. Without this, the loading state was too brief to screenshot since the API is local and responds in <100ms.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser network route with --delay 5000 to slow down API responses, allowing the loading spinner to be captured

frictions.description: Capturing the error state (VAL-DASH-011) required navigating to about:blank first to clear React Query cache, then setting abort routes before navigating to the dashboard. Simply aborting routes on an already-loaded page did not trigger the error because React Query served cached data.
frictions.resolution: Navigated to about:blank to clear cache, set network abort routes on API endpoints, then navigated to dashboard which correctly showed the error state

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. VAL-DASH-009: Empty state shows context-aware messages (filter vs no-task). VAL-DASH-010: Loading state displays centered spinner with 'Loading board...' text. VAL-DASH-011: Error state shows warning icon, 'Connection Error' message, and working 'Retry' button. VAL-DASH-012: Responsive layout stacks columns vertically at 375px viewport width.
